title: grindelia tricuspis
intro: Grindelia tricuspis, also known as gumweed, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it's valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Grindelia tricuspis include relief from allergies, asthma, and other respiratory issues, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Grindelia tricuspis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in salves and ointments for topical application.
